skill.name: startup-metrics-framework
skill.description: Guides startup teams through key SaaS, marketplace, consumer, and B2B metrics with formulas, benchmarks, and reporting practices.
skill.summary: Build a focused startup metrics framework with stage-specific benchmarks and unit economics.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown formula blocks and business terminology as command execution, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance. Manual review found no executable code, no network activity, no filesystem access, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown formulas flagged as external commands
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur in Markdown code fences containing startup metric formulas and dashboard examples. No shell command, Ruby execution, subprocess call, or user-controlled command execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is enclosed as Markdown examples or inline reference paths. It contains formulas and documentation text, not executable code.

low_findings.title: False positive: Business terms flagged as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are word-match false positives in descriptive startup metrics text. The file does not implement cryptographic functions or recommend cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only natural language about metrics and marketplace balance at the flagged lines. No cryptographic API, hash function, or cipher usage appears.

low_findings.title: False positive: Benchmark comparison flagged as network reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to a benchmark line for net dollar retention. There is no scanner, host lookup, port probe, URL, or network request behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a SaaS retention benchmark, not a command or network operation. No adjacent content introduces reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Startup teams often track too many metrics or miss the few that explain growth quality. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure metrics by business model, stage, and investor context.
actual_capabilities: Explains core revenue, retention, cash efficiency, and unit economics formulas.
Provides benchmark ranges for SaaS, marketplace, consumer, mobile, and B2B models.
Maps priority metrics to pre-seed, seed, Series A, and later startup stages.
Guides investor-ready metric presentation for fundraising and board updates.
Highlights common tracking mistakes such as vanity metrics and weak segmentation.
limitations: Does not connect to analytics systems or calculate metrics from live company data.
Benchmarks are general guidance and may not fit every market, geography, or pricing model.
Does not replace financial, accounting, legal, or investment advice.
Reference and example files named in the guide are not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Define Seed-Stage Metrics
use_cases.description: Choose a small set of metrics for traction updates, runway planning, and early unit economics.

use_cases.title: Prepare Investor Reporting
use_cases.description: Structure monthly or quarterly updates around ARR, retention, burn multiple, runway, and efficiency.

use_cases.title: Build Operating Dashboards
use_cases.description: Translate SaaS, marketplace, consumer, or B2B goals into focused dashboard sections and review cadences.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Core Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: We are a [business model] startup at [stage]. Help us choose 5 to 7 core metrics, explain why each matters, and define a weekly review cadence.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Unit Economics
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these inputs: ARPU [amount], gross margin [percent], monthly churn [percent], CAC [amount], and sales cycle [days]. Calculate LTV, LTV:CAC, CAC payback, and key risks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Board Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise board metrics narrative for our [stage] company using MRR, ARR growth, NDR, burn, runway, and pipeline data.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Growth Efficiency
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess our growth efficiency using burn multiple, magic number, Rule of 40, retention, and cohort trends. Identify the three most important operating changes.

output_examples.input: A seed SaaS company asks which metrics to track before fundraising.
output_examples.output: A focused list of MRR growth, gross retention, CAC baseline, LTV:CAC, burn rate, runway, and product engagement with benchmark context.

output_examples.input: A marketplace team wants to understand growth quality.
output_examples.output: A metrics plan covering GMV, take rate, fill rate, repeat rate, supply-demand balance, and signs of liquidity risk.

output_examples.input: A Series A company needs an investor dashboard format.
output_examples.output: A concise dashboard outline showing ARR, growth rate, CAC payback, net dollar retention, burn multiple, runway, and trend commentary.

best_practices: Start with the business model and company stage before selecting metrics.
Track a small core set consistently and segment results by channel, customer type, and cohort.
Pair every metric with a target, benchmark, trend, and decision owner.
anti_patterns: Using vanity metrics such as total users without activation, retention, or revenue context.
Scaling paid acquisition before CAC payback and retention are understood.
Reporting dozens of metrics without a clear operating decision tied to each one.

faq.question: Which business models does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers SaaS, marketplaces, consumer and mobile products, and B2B sales-led companies.

faq.question: Can it calculate metrics from my raw data?
faq.answer: It can explain calculations and review provided inputs, but it does not connect to live data sources.

faq.question: Is this useful before revenue?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes pre-seed guidance focused on retention, engagement, product-market fit, and early learning.

faq.question: Does it include investor benchmarks?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists common benchmark ranges for growth, retention, CAC payback, burn multiple, and efficiency metrics.

faq.question: Can this replace a finance model?
faq.answer: No. It supports metric selection and interpretation, but it does not replace a full finance model.

faq.question: How should teams use it with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Provide company stage, business model, metric inputs, and reporting goal, then ask for focused analysis or a dashboard outline.
